perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement: (a) the terms "Affiliate" and "Associate" shall have the respective meanings set forth in Rule 12b-2 promulgated by the SEC under the Exchange Act and shall include all persons or entities that at any time during the term of this Agreement become Affiliates or Associates of any person or entity referred to in this Agreement; (b) the term "Exchange Act" shall mean the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended; and (c) the terms "person" or "persons" mean any individual, corporation (including not-for-profit), general or limited partnership, limited liability or unlimited liability company, joint venture, estate, trust, association, organization or other entity of any kind or nature.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement: (a) the terms "Affiliate" and "Associate" shall have the respective meanings set forth in Rule 12b-2 promulgated by the SEC under the Exchange Act and shall only include those persons or entities that, as of the date of this agreement, are already Affiliates or Associates of any person or entity referred to in this Agreement; (b) the term "Exchange Act" shall mean the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended; and (c) the terms "person" or "persons" mean any individual, corporation (including not-for-profit), general or limited partnership, limited liability or unlimited liability company, joint venture, estate, trust, association, organization or other entity of any kind or nature.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text **creates an in-text contradiction** by changing the definition of "Affiliate" and "Associate" to exclude any entities that become affiliates or associates *after* the agreement is signed. This contradicts earlier sections of the contract, such as Section 2(a), which states "The MG Capital Parties shall comply, and shall cause each of their Affiliates and Associates (as hereinafter defined) to comply with the terms of this Agreement and shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such Affiliate or Associate." Section 2(a) relies on a definition of Affiliate that includes entities becoming Affiliates at *any* time during the term of the agreement. By restricting the definition, it is now unclear which Affiliates the MG Capital Parties are responsible for, leading to potential disputes and uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The MG Capital Parties shall comply, and shall cause each of their Affiliates and Associates (as hereinafter defined) to comply with the terms of this Agreement and shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such Affiliate or Associate.
perturbation.justification: The modified text redefines "Affiliate" and "Associate" to only include those entities that are affiliates/associates as of the date of the agreement. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that "The MG Capital Parties shall comply, and shall cause each of their Affiliates and Associates (as hereinafter defined) to comply with the terms of this Agreement and shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such Affiliate or Associate." The modified definition means that the MG Capital Parties are only responsible for the actions of entities that were affiliates/associates at the time of signing. This conflicts with the original intent of the contract (as evidenced by the original text), as the contradicted text indicates that the parties intended to be responsible for affiliates/associates who become affiliated at any point during the agreement. This creates a direct conflict, making it an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Within five (5) business days following receipt of reasonably satisfactory documentation thereof, the Company will reimburse the MG Capital Parties for their fees and expenses (including all legal, public relations, proxy advisory and out-of-pocket expenses, the "Expenses") incurred in preparation for and in connection with the matters relating to the consent solicitation run by the MG Capital Parties, the 2020 Annual Meeting and the negotiation, execution and effectuation of this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby, in an amount equal to $352,290.25 (the "Initial Reimbursement"). Between the date of the Initial Reimbursement and the date of the 2020 Annual Meeting, the Company will reimburse the MG Capital Parties for the Expenses in an aggregate amount not exceeding $650,000 (inclusive of the Initial Reimbursement, the "Cap"), which Expenses shall be reimbursed on a dollar-for-dollar basis at the same time as the Company reimburses its third party vendors (e.g., legal counsel, public relations firm, financial advisor and proxy advisory firm)in connection with the MG Capital Parties' consent solicitation and Nomination Notice; provided that all Expenses, subject to the Cap, shall be reimbursed no later than the date of the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: Within five (5) business days following receipt of reasonably satisfactory documentation thereof, the Company will reimburse the MG Capital Parties for their fees and expenses (including all legal, public relations, proxy advisory and out-of-pocket expenses, the "Expenses") incurred in preparation for and in connection with the matters relating to the consent solicitation run by the MG Capital Parties, the 2020 Annual Meeting and the negotiation, execution and effectuation of this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby, in an amount equal to $352,290.25 (the "Initial Reimbursement"). The Company will not reimburse any expenses beyond the Initial Reimbursement amount.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation **creates an in-text contradiction** by modifying Section 10 to state that the company will *not* reimburse any expenses beyond the initial reimbursement. This directly contradicts the original language which states, "Between the date of the Initial Reimbursement and the date of the 2020 Annual Meeting, the Company will reimburse the MG Capital Parties for the Expenses in an aggregate amount not exceeding $650,000." The new language introduces uncertainty around expense reimbursement, leading to potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Within five (5) business days following receipt of reasonably satisfactory documentation thereof, the Company will reimburse the MG Capital Parties for their fees and expenses (including all legal, public relations, proxy advisory and out-of-pocket expenses, the "Expenses") incurred in preparation for and in connection with the matters relating to the consent solicitation run by the MG Capital Parties, the 2020 Annual Meeting and the negotiation, execution and effectuation of this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby, in an amount equal to $352,290.25 (the "Initial Reimbursement"). Between the date of the Initial Reimbursement and the date of the 2020 Annual Meeting, the Company will reimburse the MG Capital Parties for the Expenses in an aggregate amount not exceeding $650,000 (inclusive of the Initial Reimbursement, the "Cap"), which Expenses shall be reimbursed on a dollar-for-dollar basis at the same time as the Company reimburses its third party vendors (e.g., legal counsel, public relations firm, financial advisor and proxy advisory firm)in connection with the MG Capital Parties' consent solicitation and Nomination Notice; provided that all Expenses, subject to the Cap, shall be reimbursed no later than the date of the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "The Company will not reimburse any expenses beyond the Initial Reimbursement amount," which directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, which both state "Between the date of the Initial Reimbursement and the date of the 2020 Annual Meeting, the Company will reimburse the MG Capital Parties for the Expenses in an aggregate amount not exceeding $650,000." This creates a direct conflict regarding expense reimbursement, meeting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will terminate upon the earlier of: (i) the conclusion of the Standstill Period or (ii) delivery of written notice by one Party to the other Party of a material breach of this Agreement by the breaching Party that is uncured after ten (10) calendar days of notice of such breach. Upon such termination, this Agreement shall have no further force and effect. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Sections 9 through Section 19 hereof shall survive termination of this Agreement, and no termination of this Agreement shall relieve any party of liability for any breach of this Agreement arising prior to such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will terminate upon the earlier of: (i) the conclusion of the Standstill Period or (ii) delivery of written notice by one Party to the other Party of a material breach of this Agreement by the breaching Party that is uncured after ten (10) calendar days of notice of such breach. Upon such termination, this Agreement shall have no further force and effect. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Sections 9 through Section 19 hereof shall not survive termination of this Agreement, and no termination of this Agreement shall relieve any party of liability for any breach of this Agreement arising prior to such termination.
perturbation.explanation: This modification **creates an in-text contradiction** within Section 13. The original clause states that certain sections (9-19) *survive* termination, while the modified clause states that those sections will *not* survive termination. This creates direct uncertainty regarding which provisions of the agreement remain in effect after termination. It is unclear whether sections like those concerning notices, expenses, specific performance, and more, will still be enforceable after termination. It also causes contradiction for litigation, as it is unclear whether the breaching party will be held accountable, after the termination of this aggreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will terminate upon the earlier of: (i) the conclusion of the Standstill Period or (ii) delivery of written notice by one Party to the other Party of a material breach of this Agreement by the breaching Party that is uncured after ten (10) calendar days of notice of such breach. Upon such termination, this Agreement shall have no further force and effect. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Sections 9 through Section 19 hereof shall survive termination of this Agreement, and no termination of this Agreement shall relieve any party of liability for any breach of this Agreement arising prior to such termination.
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts the original text regarding the survival of Sections 9 through 19 after termination of the agreement. The original text states they *shall* survive, while the modified text states they *shall not* survive. This creates a clear and undeniable in-text contradiction within the document itself, leading to uncertainty about the enforceability of those sections after termination.
